meaning: the Quran (as the object of عَلَّمَ/taught)
he taught him (object of the teaching in الْبَيَانَ - clear speech)
the clear explanation (object of عَلَّمَهُ - He taught it)
and the moon (as the subject with ٱلشَّمْسُ)
by/with a precise calculation (describing how ٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ move)
they both prostrate (referring to النَّجْمُ وَٱلشَّجَرُ - the star and tree)
and the sky (object of رَفَعَهَا - He raised it)
he raised it (referring to السَّمَآءَ/the sky)
the balance/scale (object of وَضَعَ - He set/placed)
you all cross limits (don't cross limits in ٱلْمِيزَانِ)
and establish! (all of you) (command to establish الوزن)
with justice (used to describe how they should establish وَزْن/weighing)
and the earth (object of وَضَعَهَا/He laid it)
he placed it/her (referring to الْأَرْضَ, the earth)
and the grain (which is described by ذُو ٱلْعَصْفِ)
so by/with which (asking about specific favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
of the favors (possessed by رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you two deny intensely (referring to humans and jinn in this ayah)
he created (subject of the sentence, followed by ٱلْإِنسٰنَ - mankind)
of dry clay that makes a ringing sound (describing الْإِنسٰنَ/mankind)
like the pottery (comparing humans to this material in the ayah)
and he created (subject of creating الْجَآنَّ)
from (emphasized because it's followed by نَّارٍ)
you two strongly deny (referring to humans and jinn in this ayah)
The Lord (of الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ - the two Easts)
so by which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
you both deny (referring to humans and jinn in this ayah)
He released/let flow (referring to the two seas يَلْتَقِيَانِ that meet)
the two seas (object of مَرَجَ - He released)
they both meet with each other (referring to ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ - the two seas)
between them both (referring to the two bodies of water mentioned earlier)
they both transgress (referring to the two seas)
you two deny intensely/repeatedly (addressing both humans and jinn)
from both of them (source of يَخْرُجُ)
the pearl (object of يَخْرُجُ - comes out)
so by which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
blessings, favors (belonging to رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you two are denying (referring to humans and jinn in this ayah)
and to Him belong (the ships in the next phrase)
the ships (that are mentioned in ٱلْمُنشَـَٔاتُ)
the ones brought into existence (referring to ships in ٱلْبَحْرِ)
like the mountains (referring to ships that appear as tall as mountains in the sea)
then by which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
your (both of you) Lord (addressing both humans and jinn in this ayah)
you both strongly deny/reject (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
all, every (describes مَنْ 'whoever' in مَنْ عَلَيْهَا فَانٍ)
upon her/it (referring to everything on Earth)
you two strongly deny/reject (addressing both humans and jinn)
he asks him (subject of asks من 'whoever')
in/within (in ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the heavens and earth)
and of the earth (connected to مَن فِى)
He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
the complete nurturer/sustainer of you both (addressing both jinn and humans)
you two deny/reject repeatedly (addressing both jinn and humans)
we will finish with (لَكُمْ) you all
the two weighty ones (referring to humans and jinn)
favors, blessings (possessed by رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you both deny intensely (addressing both Allah's creations mentioned in this surah)
the jinn (object of يٰمَعْشَرَ/O assembly of)
and the humankind (possessed by/connected to something)
then pass through! (command to many people)
so by which (of the favors of your Lord do you both deny?)
of the blessings (possessed by رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
upon you two (being sent against in يُرْسَلُ)
a smokeless flame (subject of يُرْسَلُ - is sent)
from (shows what شُوَاظٌ 'flame' is made of)
of intense fire (part of مِّن نَّارٍ - of intense fire)
so by which (asking about favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
you two deny/reject intensively (addressing both jinn and humans)
and when/then when (introduces ٱنشَقَّتِ - split apart)
she split apart by herself (referring to السَّمَآءُ/the sky)
the sky (subject of ٱنشَقَّتِ - split apart)
and then she was/became (subject of وَرْدَةً)
like the oils (describing the sky when it splits)
gifts and favors (of رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
your (both of you) lord (subject of تُكَذِّبَانِ)
so on that day (connects to لَّا يُسْـَٔلُ - no one will be asked)
so by/with which (asking about specific favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
you two deny/reject (addressing both Allah's favors mentioned in رَبِّكُمَا)
he is recognized (subject of this verb is ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ - the criminals)
the criminals (who will be recognized by their marks)
by their marks (they will be recognized by these marks)
by/with the forelocks (object of يُؤْخَذُ - they will be seized)
and the feet (objects being grabbed along with النواصي)
your (both of you) Lord (addressing both humans and jinn)
you two deny intensively/repeatedly (addressing both humans and jinn)
he denies strongly (the criminals deny جَهَنَّمُ)
the criminals (who are being warned about Hell)
between it/her (referring to the boiling water mentioned after)
and between (connecting to بَيْنَهَا in previous phrase)
intensely hot water (being acted upon in يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ)
you two deny strongly (addressing both humans and jinn)
place of standing (object of خَافَ - feared)
his one who cares for and helps others grow (possessed by مَقَامَ)
so by/with which (of the favors of your Lord do you both deny?)
you two deny intensely (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
two possessors of (feminine dual, describing أَفْنَانٍ)
so by which (asking about means/method in response to something) (connected to تُكَذِّبَانِ)
your (both of you) Lord (addressing both humans and jinn)
you two deny strongly/repeatedly (addressing both Allah's creations mentioned in this surah: humans and jinn)
in both of them (referring to the two gardens mentioned earlier)
they both flow (referring to عَيْنَانِ - two springs)
you two deny intensely (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
in both of them (referring to the two gardens)
from/of (connects فٰكِهَةٍ to what's before it)
your (both of you) lord (addressing two beings)
you two deny strongly/repeatedly (addressing both humans and jinn)
those who are reclining (describes the people of Paradise)
on/upon (describing reclining مُتَّكِـِٔينَ on فُرُشٍۭ luxurious couches)
from/of (describing what فُرُشٍۭ is made of)
of thick silk brocade (describing فُرُشٍ - cushions)
and gathered fruit (describing the fruit in الْجَنَّتَيْنِ - the two gardens)
you two strongly deny/reject (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
in them (referring to the gardens mentioned earlier)
the glance (owned by قٰصِرٰتُ - those who lower)
he touches them (feminine plural) intimately (referring to the women mentioned before)
you two strongly deny (addressing both Allah's creation - mankind and jinn)
as if they (feminine plural) (describing the women of paradise being compared to ٱلْيَاقُوتُ وَٱلْمَرْجَانُ)
the ruby/sapphire (subject being compared to in كَأَنَّهُنَّ)
and the coral (which is like rubies)
you two deny/reject (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
the doing of good (subject of جَزَآءُ)
so with which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
blessings and gifts (belonging to رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you two deny repeatedly (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
below them both (referring to the two gardens mentioned after)
then by which (of the blessings of رَبِّكُمَا)
you two deny intensively/repeatedly (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
multiple gifts and blessings (of رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you two deny intensely (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
in both of them (referring to عَيْنَانِ/two springs)
then by which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
you two deny intensely (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
in both of them (referring to the two gardens mentioned earlier)
so by which (asking about specific things in response to what was mentioned)
blessings and favors (belonging to رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
so with which (of the favors of رَبِّكُمَا)
the many gifts and blessings (of رَبِّكُمَا)
you two deny repeatedly/strongly (referring to humans and jinn being addressed in this surah)
fair ones with beautiful wide eyes (describing companions in Paradise)
you two strongly deny/reject (addressing both humans and jinn in this ayah)
not (changes يَطْمِثْهُنَّ from present to past)
humans/mankind (as the subject of لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ)
before them (referring to the humans mentioned in إِنسٌ)
so by which (asking about means/method in response to something)
favors/blessings of (connects to رَبِّكُمَا - your Lord)
you both deny intensely (addressing both humans and jinn in this ayah)
upon/on (describing the position of مُتَّكِـِٔينَ - reclining)
beautiful ones (describing the cushions mentioned earlier)
you two deny/reject intensively (addressing both of you in فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا)
He is supremely blessed/exalted (referring to اللَّهِ)
and the honoring (possessed by رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
